title: Model strategic reaction using same-lap field context and pit-wave features
summary: Public F1 strategy papers emphasize that competition changes optimal stopping behavior, and recent pit-stop decision support finds pitting probability rises when nearby cars pit.
rationale: Public F1 strategy papers emphasize that competition changes optimal stopping behavior, and recent pit-stop decision support finds pitting probability rises when nearby cars pit. The dataset likely allows same-lap race context from all cars in a race-lap snapshot.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-LapNumber group, create features such as fraction of cars already pitted this lap, whether adjacent on-track positions pitted, position-relative pace z-scores, and local cluster signals by front/mid/back of field. Test both raw group stats and interaction terms with compound, stint, and race phase.
expected_effect: Captures undercut/cover reactions that single-car features miss, likely lifting AUC on borderline strategic laps.
risk: Need careful definition of what is observable at prediction time; avoid using any information that would not exist before the next-lap decision.
